{"society_power": 4.0, "state_power": 3.0, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "French Mandate creates Greater Lebanon (1920)", "reasoning": "After Ottoman collapse and the 1920 establishment of Greater Lebanon under French Mandate, central administration was nascent and heavily dependent on France and sectarian notables, giving it only weak reach. Society was organized largely along confessional and clientelist lines with active religious communities, an emerging press, and merchant elites, producing meaningful but fragmented mobilization capacity."}
